# @tarsk/mastodon

> Command-line interface for Mastodon. Run with `npx @tarsk/mastodon`. Built on masto.js.

@tarsk/mastodon is a Node.js CLI (requires Node >= 20) for posting statuses, reading timelines, checking your account, and searching Mastodon instances. Authentication uses environment variables only — no config files or OAuth flow.

## Setup

Create an access token on your Mastodon instance:

1. Go to Preferences → Development → New Application
2. Set an application name (other fields can stay as defaults)
3. Enable scopes: `read` and `write`
4. Copy **Your access token**

Set required environment variables before every command:

```bash
export MASTODON_URL=https://mastodon.social
export MASTODON_TOKEN=your_token_here
```

- `MASTODON_URL` — instance base URL (trailing slashes are stripped)
- `MASTODON_TOKEN` — personal access token from step above

If either variable is missing, the CLI exits with an error message.

## Invocation

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon [command] [args] [options]
```

Local development (from repo root):

```bash
npm run build
node dist/cli.js [command] [args] [options]
```

Show help:

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon --help
npx @tarsk/mastodon help
```

## Global options

- `--json` — output raw JSON to stdout (works on all commands)
- `--help`, `-h` — print usage and exit

## Default behavior

If the first argument is not a known subcommand, all arguments are treated as a `post` command. These are equivalent:

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon "Hello #fediverse"
npx @tarsk/mastodon post "Hello #fediverse"
```

## Commands

### post

Publish a status. On success, prints the status URL to stdout (unless `--json`).

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon post "Hello world"
npx @tarsk/mastodon post "Photo post" --media ./cat.png --alt "A cat"
npx @tarsk/mastodon post "Reply" --reply-to 123456789
npx @tarsk/mastodon post "DM" --visibility direct
npx @tarsk/mastodon post "Multi-image" --media a.png --media b.png --alt "First" --alt "Second"
```

Options:

- `--media path` — local file to attach (repeatable, max 4 attachments)
- `--alt text` — alt text / media description (repeatable; if fewer alt texts than media, the first alt text is reused)
- `--visibility public|unlisted|private|direct` — default: `public`
- `--reply-to id` — status ID to reply to
- `--json` — output full status object as JSON

Post text is taken from positional arguments joined by spaces.

### timeline

Fetch statuses from a timeline.

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon timeline
npx @tarsk/mastodon timeline --type home --limit 5
npx @tarsk/mastodon timeline --type public --limit 10
npx @tarsk/mastodon timeline --type local --limit 10
npx @tarsk/mastodon timeline --json
```

Options:

- `--type home|public|local` — default: `home`
  - `home` — statuses from followed users
  - `public` — federated public timeline
  - `local` — public timeline filtered to local statuses only
- `--limit N` — number of statuses to fetch (default: 20)
- `--json` — output array of status objects as JSON

Human-readable output format per status:

```
@username · 2h
Post text snippet...
https://instance/@user/123456789
```

### whoami

Show the authenticated account.

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon whoami
npx @tarsk/mastodon whoami --json
```

Human-readable output: `@acct · display name · bio snippet`

### search

Search the instance.

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon search "typescript"
npx @tarsk/mastodon search "@user" --type accounts
npx @tarsk/mastodon search "fediverse" --type hashtags
npx @tarsk/mastodon search "hello world" --type statuses --json
```

Options:

- `--type accounts|hashtags|statuses` — default: `statuses`
- `--json` — output full search result object as JSON

Query is taken from positional arguments joined by spaces. Returns up to 20 results.

Human-readable output groups results under Accounts, Hashtags, and Statuses headings.

## Output modes

| Command  | Default output              | With `--json`        |
|----------|-----------------------------|----------------------|
| post     | Status URL                  | Full status object   |
| timeline | Formatted status list       | Array of statuses    |
| whoami   | `@acct · name · bio`        | Full account object  |
| search   | Grouped human-readable list | Full search object   |

Errors are written to stderr. The CLI exits with code 1 on errors.

## Examples for automation

Post and capture the URL:

```bash
URL=$(npx @tarsk/mastodon post "Automated post")
echo "Posted: $URL"
```

Fetch timeline as JSON for scripting:

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon timeline --type home --limit 5 --json | jq '.[].content'
```

Verify credentials:

```bash
npx @tarsk/mastodon whoami --json | jq -r '.acct'
```

## Limitations (v1)

Not supported in this version:

- Config file or `auth` subcommand
- OAuth browser flow
- Follow, unfollow, favourite, boost, delete, notifications
- Streaming timelines

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md): Quick start and command summary
- [masto.js](https://github.com/neet/masto.js): Underlying Mastodon API client
- [Mastodon API docs](https://docs.joinmastodon.org/api/): Official API reference
